description: Sentry + Linear 错误监控与自动同步 — iOS 崩溃捕获、性能追踪、Sentry Webhook → GitHub Actions → Linear Issue 全链路自动化

userStories.title: 集成 Sentry iOS SDK
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the Sentry SDK initialized at app launch so that all unhandled crashes and errors are automatically captured.
acceptanceCriteria: Add Swift Package Manager dependency: https://github.com/getsentry/sentry-cocoa version >= 8.36.0 to DayPage.xcodeproj
Link Sentry library to DayPage target
Add static let sentryDSN: String to GeneratedSecrets.swift (empty string placeholder)
Add SENTRY_DSN entry to scripts/generate_secrets.sh following same pattern as existing keys
Add SENTRY_DSN= placeholder line to .env.example with comment

In DayPageApp.swift init, call SentrySDK.start { options in options.dsn = GeneratedSecrets.sentryDSN; options.tracesSampleRate = ProcessInfo.processInfo.environment["DEBUG"] != nil ? 1.0 : 0.2; options.enableCrashHandler = true; options.attachScreenshot = true; options.attachViewHierarchy = true }
SentrySDK.start is only called when sentryDSN is non-empty (guard against empty DSN)
xcodebuild -scheme DayPage build passes
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: 性能监控 — API 追踪 Span 封装
userStories.description: As a developer, I want Sentry to trace outbound API calls so that slow compilations and Whisper timeouts are visible in Sentry Performance.
acceptanceCriteria: In CompilationService.swift, wrap the DashScope URLSession request in a Sentry span: let span = SentrySDK.startTransaction(name: "compilation.dashscope", operation: "http.client"); defer { span.finish() }
In VoiceService.swift, wrap the Whisper API URLSession request in a Sentry span with name: "voice.whisper", operation: "http.client"
In WeatherService.swift, wrap the OpenWeatherMap URLSession request in a Sentry span with name: "weather.fetch", operation: "http.client"
In BackgroundCompilationService.swift, wrap background task execution in a Sentry transaction named "background.compilation" and call SentrySDK.flush(timeout: 5) before task completion handler
Spans are only created when SentrySDK is initialized (sentryDSN non-empty)
xcodebuild -scheme DayPage build passes
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: DayPageLogger 与 Sentry Breadcrumb 打通
userStories.description: As a developer, I want existing log entries to appear as Sentry breadcrumbs so that I can see what the user did before a crash without changing all call sites.
acceptanceCriteria: Find DayPageLogger.swift (or equivalent logging utility) in the codebase

In DayPageLogger.error(), add: let crumb = Breadcrumb(level: .error, category: "app"); crumb.message = message; SentrySDK.addBreadcrumb(crumb); also call SentrySDK.capture(message: message, level: .error)
In DayPageLogger.warn() (or warning()), add Sentry breadcrumb with level: .warning
In DayPageLogger.info(), add Sentry breadcrumb with level: .info
Breadcrumb message uses format consistent with existing log output
All existing DayPageLogger call sites compile without modification
Sentry calls are no-ops when SDK not initialized (SDK handles this gracefully by default)
xcodebuild -scheme DayPage build passes
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: 用户上下文注入
userStories.description: As a developer, I want Sentry events to include user ID and app version so I can filter errors by user cohort and build.
acceptanceCriteria: In AuthService.swift, after successful sign-in (session becomes non-nil), call: let user = SentrySDK.setUser(SentryUser(userId: session.user.id.uuidString))
In AuthService.signOut(), call SentrySDK.setUser(nil)
If AuthService does not exist yet (auth not integrated), add the Sentry user calls to a placeholder location with a TODO comment noting where to wire them when auth is added
Verify Sentry automatically captures app.version (CFBundleShortVersionString), app.build (CFBundleVersion), device.model, os.version via SDK defaults — no manual configuration needed
xcodebuild -scheme DayPage build passes
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: GitHub Actions Sentry→Linear 中转 Workflow
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a GitHub Actions workflow that receives Sentry webhooks and creates Linear issues so the pipeline is version-controlled and auditable.
acceptanceCriteria: Create .github/workflows/sentry-to-linear.yml triggered by repository_dispatch with event type sentry_issue
Workflow reads from event payload: issue_id, title, culprit, url, count, userCount, environment, fingerprint
Step 1 — Dedup check: query Linear GraphQL API for open issues where title starts with '[Sentry]' and description contains the fingerprint; if found, skip creation and exit with success
Step 2 — Priority mapping: userCount >= 10 → priority 1 (Urgent), >= 3 → priority 2 (High), else → priority 3 (Medium)

Step 3 — Create Linear issue via GraphQL mutation issueCreate with: title = '[Sentry] {title}', description includes error summary + culprit + affected users + occurrence count + environment + Sentry URL + fingerprint, labelIds = bug label, priority from mapping, teamId from LINEAR_TEAM_ID secret
Workflow uses secrets: LINEAR_API_KEY, LINEAR_TEAM_ID, SENTRY_WEBHOOK_SECRET
Add workflow-level permissions: contents: read
On failure, workflow exits with non-zero code so GitHub Actions sends notification
Add comments in the YAML explaining each step
Typecheck passes (YAML lint via yamllint or actionlint if available, otherwise skip)

userStories.title: Sentry Webhook 配置 Runbook
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a runbook documenting how to configure the Sentry Webhook so any team member can set up or restore the pipeline.
acceptanceCriteria: Create tasks/runbook-sentry-linear.md
Runbook section 1: Prerequisites — list required accounts (Sentry, Linear, GitHub), required secrets (SENTRY_DSN, LINEAR_API_KEY, LINEAR_TEAM_ID, SENTRY_WEBHOOK_SECRET), and how to obtain each

Runbook section 2: Sentry Webhook setup steps — navigate to Sentry Project Settings → Integrations → Webhooks, add endpoint URL format: https://api.github.com/repos/{owner}/{repo}/dispatches with Authorization header using GitHub PAT, configure to trigger on New Issue event only
Runbook section 3: GitHub Secrets setup — list all required secrets and where to set them (repo Settings → Secrets → Actions)
Runbook section 4: Testing the pipeline — curl command to manually trigger repository_dispatch with sample sentry_issue payload for testing without real Sentry events
Runbook section 5: Verification checklist — empty checkboxes for operator to fill in after setup
Typecheck passes
